non_blocking_notes.title: Boundary verified: metadata-only, fully fail-closed approval routing
non_blocking_notes.description: Claude verified that the S15 prechecker is pure frozen-result construction with no I/O, fails closed on wrong source, missing audit evidence, missing receipt, denied or unapplied S14 review, forbidden claims, and unknown truthy claims, keeps all write/runtime/approval flags false, leaves the source microphase open, and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S16.

non_blocking_notes.title: Duplicate entries in forbidden approval-required routing claims
non_blocking_notes.description: Claude noted that two inherited forbidden claim names appear twice after spreading the S14 forbidden list and re-appending approval-related claims. This is functionally harmless because the precheck uses a Set and the policy, contract, and fixture mirror the same ordering. Optional hygiene cleanup only.

non_blocking_notes.title: Inherited required_evidence_refs naming
non_blocking_notes.description: Claude noted that required_evidence_refs retain claude_review_required_routing names from the shared baseline used by prior S01-S14 packs. This was not introduced by CP00-011 and is not boundary drift.

adjudication_notes: One completed claude-opus-4-8 max read-only/no-tools review is recorded for CP00-011.
Claude returned approve/PASS with zero P0, zero P1, and zero P2 findings.
The duplicate forbidden-claim note is non-blocking and deferred to a later hygiene pack because changing implementation after review would change the reviewed diff without fixing a blocking risk.
The required_evidence_refs naming note is a pre-existing shared-baseline convention and requires no action in CP00-011.
The expected pre-evidence RP00 failure is resolved by creating this CP00-011 evidence and rerunning final validation.
